mode_reason: Pre-flight checks: no pending operator directives; pipeline health green (no stalled agents, FUSE matches GitHub for all checked data files, auth OK); backlog metrics healthy (-82% WoW). Two stranded-patches files >24h old were noted but are operator-pending and not blocking. Last 5+ tinker runs were directive-overrides; Mode 3 work has not gotten airtime. Pipeline-healthy default → Mode 3.

Caveat: FUSE workspace-sync.md lags HEAD by ~24 lines (missing PROP-045 anti-reversion guard) — propagation deferred to next build.js publish.

modes_checked.proposals: 0 pending self-fixes; 1 latent inconsistency (calibration-audits.jsonl shape vs NEVER_PUSH classification) — captured as finding, no PROP yet

output_freshness.social: not checked individually this run; queue+freshness via prior tinker reports clean

stranded_patches_gt_24h.applicability_notes: Of the 4 build-scripts/generate-html.js patches, NONE have their find text present in the current file (file has changed since patches were generated 2026-05-14). The 2 test.js patches have both find and replace text present (replace contains find — already-applied semantics ambiguous). The 3 docs/index.html entries have no find/replace — they reference EXP-386 patches[1..2] and EXP-387 patches[1] via 'note' fields.
Both find AND replace text are absent from current generate-html.js — same staleness pattern.

findings.title: Pre-flight metrics healthy; no thresholds firing
findings.description: Open-issues total 37 (was 207 a week ago → −82.1% WoW). 7-day velocity: 116 closed vs 6 new (net +110). assigned-analyst=16 (well below moderate threshold of 50). age_ge_30d=1, age_ge_14d=20. Baby-drain and verify-drain both 0 this 7-day window — flagging the latter for note but not for alert (verify pipeline new; cadence every-4h means recent runs cluster).

findings.title: Stranded patches >24h: 2 files, 14 of 15 source ISSs still open
findings.description: Both stranded-patches files were created by decider for PROP-016 Mech A (patches targeting NEVER_PUSH files: build-scripts/generate-html.js, test.js). Operator must apply manually in own clone. Both files are 60h+ old, no tombstones. 14 of 15 stranded ISSs are still OPEN in the issue tracker. ADDITIONAL CONCERN: for the build-scripts/generate-html.js patches in the 14T21 file (4 patches) AND in the 15T01 file (2 patches), neither find text nor replace text is currently present in the workspace's generate-html.js — meaning the surrounding code has been rewritten since 2026-05-14. The patches likely need manual re-targeting (or are obsolete) before they can be applied.
findings.evidence: monitor/decisions/stranded-patches-2026-05-14T21-28-05Z.json (9 patches), monitor/decisions/stranded-patches-2026-05-15T01-29-11Z.json (2 patches); cross-referenced against current generate-html.js, test.js, docs/index.html, and open-issues.json/closed-issues.json. Operator-action-needed not pipeline broken.
findings.recommended_operator_action: Inspect both stranded-patches files. Either (a) re-target the obsolete patches against the current generate-html.js and apply, or (b) mark them tombstone_status='obsolete' with rationale, and reopen the 14 remaining OPEN ISSs for re-triage (decider should re-process from the current source).

findings.title: FUSE workspace-sync.md lags HEAD (~24 lines, missing PROP-045 anti-reversion guard)
findings.description: FUSE monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md is 863L (md5 1a95eab9); GitHub main is 887L (md5 b003c762). The 24-line delta is the PROP-045 anti-reversion guard added in commit history today. workspace-sync agents read their prompt from FUSE, so until the next 'node build.js publish' runs, workspace-sync executes the pre-PROP-045 code path. The smart_copy in FUSE applies the old mtime-only guard. NOT urgent — anti-reversion is a defense-in-depth measure, not a critical-path fix, and the propagation-after-publish pattern is by design for prompts (NEVER_PUSH, dynamic rule).
findings.evidence: md5sum FUSE vs curl raw.githubusercontent.com; diff shows the new anti-reversion block at lines 177-204 and the documentation paragraph at line 875.
findings.recommended_operator_action: Run `node build.js publish` from the clean clone (or no-op publish that just syncs files) to propagate workspace-sync.md and CLAUDE.md to FUSE. Verify FUSE md5 matches main after.

findings.title: Two expansion-tracker entries (EXP-398, EXP-415) have status='complete' with no created_at/completed_at/integrated/authored_by fields
findings.description: Both entries are sparse: keys are only [id, status, category, target]. status='complete' but no integration metadata. They appear as 'stale-complete' under the Step 1b re-work scan. Not actively causing re-work, but the schema-incomplete state is unusual and may indicate a writer-side bug (analyst-baby producing minimal entries?) or a manual seed that never got filled in. Worth a glance from analyst on next BAU run.
findings.evidence: monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json; both items have only 4 keys vs typical ~12-15.
findings.recommended_operator_action: Spot-check on next analyst Mode 1 run (or operator inspection); if these were manual seeds for an aborted plan, mark them status='abandoned' or delete via operator-direct-push.

findings.title: calibration-audits.jsonl shape inconsistency with NEVER_PUSH classification
findings.description: CLAUDE.md line 128 documents monitor/sloppytoppy/calibration-audits.jsonl as 'same shape as monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl and monitor/decisions/closure-ledger.jsonl' — both of which are listed in workspace-sync.md's NEVER_PUSH array. calibration-audits.jsonl is NOT in NEVER_PUSH and IS explicitly smart_copied (FUSE → git) at workspace-sync.md line 236 (FUSE) / 259 (GH). Implication: if any non-tinker writer appends to FUSE's calibration-audits.jsonl, workspace-sync will push it. This MAY be intentional (universal-pusher rescue for tinker if its own push fails) but contradicts the 'same shape' wording in CLAUDE.md. Either the prose or the classification should be reconciled.
findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md L128; monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH array (FUSE & GH-HEAD); smart_copy line in same file.
findings.recommended_operator_action: Decide intent: (a) treat calibration-audits.jsonl as universal-pusher-rescuable (current smart_copy behavior, but reword CLAUDE.md L128 to say 'same shape as ... but classified as rescue-eligible like wins.json'), or (b) add it to NEVER_PUSH (remove the smart_copy line, treat as clone-only writes). Either is small; flag is informational.

previous_followup.evidence: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-045-*.json exists; GitHub HEAD workspace-sync.md (line 875) describes the anti-reversion guard as enforce-mode active 2026-05-17. FUSE propagation pending build.js publish (separate observation in this run's findings, not a regression of the PROP itself).

analyst.growth_breakdown: dispatcher flat (370→370). Reference growth — script includes analyst-mode0/1/1b/34/normal-analysis/infrastructure plus SCIENTIFIC-CONTEXT and DATA-SCHEMAS. Most likely driver: analyst-mode5-prospective.md was not included in the previous baseline measurement script but the methodology comment on 2026-05-16 noted it was 'optional but counted'. Need to re-verify which reference files the official script includes. Treat as MEASUREMENT-VARIANCE rather than confirmed bloat until next Mode 3 confirms the same growth with consistent inputs.

tinker.growth_breakdown: dispatcher flat (271→271); references flat; only growth is claude_md +15L (inherited). Tinker has stabilized.

structure-integrity.note: Not previously line-counted in cost-engineering. Sonnet/Haiku model — lower per-run cost. Worth a sanity check that integrity isn't ballooning.

context_load.trend: CLAUDE.md +8.2% since 2026-05-16 baseline (multiplies across all agents). Dispatcher line counts mostly stable. Reference-file growth in decider and curmudgeon paths appears to be the main driver — but exact attribution requires per-file mtime comparison which this run does not perform (one-mode-one-deep-dive rule).
alerts: claude_md: APPROACHING-MAJOR — +8.2% since 2026-05-16 (15L). Threshold for major is +15% (since it multiplies). Currently in moderate territory only because of multiplier effect; standalone +8% is not normally moderate. Recommendation: at next Mode 3 run, identify which sections grew and consider whether any extraction to monitor/prompts/reference/ is warranted.

curmudgeon: MODERATE — +17.6%, but most growth (159L) is in shared reference files (SCIENTIFIC-CONTEXT.md or DATA-SCHEMAS.md). Same growth penalty applies to analyst, decider. Single-source-of-truth investment.
decider: MODERATE — +9.4%, mostly reference files. Decider continues to be the heaviest Opus load (3247L). PROP-041 Phase 2 integration is the most likely driver.
context_load.measurement_note: Per-reference-file mtime/wc-l comparison deferred to next Mode 3 — this run was structured as cost engineering review rather than CLAUDE.md / reference-file drill-down. Numbers above are aggregate; do not overinterpret the per-file attribution.
